working: Email engagement is excellent: account-wide avg open rate 52.15% and every in-window send (42.06% to 55.71%) clears the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark, led by Alumni eNews - June 2026 at 55.71%.
Two sends drove real traffic spikes: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/19) lifted daily sessions +66.5% and the Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3) lifted +65.2% over baseline.
The Staff-Client list is the high-reach lever: 9,731 sends vs ~1,000 for Alumni sends, and it produced the single biggest lift (+66.5%).
Organic Search remains a healthy #2 channel at 806 sessions (38.3% of the 90-day mix), nearly steady vs 816 (38.4%) last run.
Highest click engagement came from Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2): click rate 1.79% and click-to-open 3.77%, well above the in-window average.
not_working: UTM leakage is the biggest data problem: all 8 in-window sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while direct_sessions_3d runs 21 to 53 (Staff-Client May: 0 email vs 53 direct; Final Reminder June: 0 vs 49). Newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so GA4 mis-credits email traffic as Direct, which is why Direct sits at 53.0% (1,115 sessions).
Several sends had negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) -50.2%, Staff-Client eNews - April 2026 -15.7%, and Alumni eNews - June 2026 (5/27) -9.8%.
Click engagement stays weak despite strong opens: in-window click rates are only 0.40% to 1.79% and click-to-open 0.88% to 3.77%; readers open but rarely click.
Organic Social is negligible at 35 sessions (1.7%), below the 5% threshold; Paid Search (1) and AI Assistant (2) are effectively zero.

GSC signals softened week-over-week: impression-weighted avg position slipped from 15.71 to 18.48, clicks fell 26 to 8, and impressions fell 3,684 to 1,687 (partly weekend/recent-day reporting lag, but worth watching).
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link in Constant Contact so GA4 credits Email instead of Direct; this is the single highest-value fix and will unmask true email-driven traffic.
Send on Tuesdays: the two biggest lifts (Staff-Client 5/19 and Final Reminder 6/3) both went out on Tuesdays, the strongest day in the window.
Repromote the Staff-Client eNews format and the Final Reminder theme, the two highest-lift sends (+66.5% and +65.2%), and prioritize the 9,731-recipient Staff-Client list for reach.
Resume cadence now: Constant Contact most_recent_send is still 6/3, roughly three weeks with no new newsletter; queue the next Alumni/Staff eNews.
Investigate the GSC position slip (15.71 to 18.48) by checking which pages lost ranking this week before concluding it is only reporting lag.
summary: All three sources (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC) are live; 90-day traffic is 2,103 sessions led by Direct (53.0%) with Organic Search second (38.3%). Email opens are strong (52.15% avg) and two Tuesday sends lifted traffic about 66%, but every send shows 0 email and 21-53 direct sessions, confirming missing UTM tags are inflating Direct and hiding email impact.
